desc: Guilin is famed for karst peaks rising above rivers and rice countryside, making it one of China’s most scenic regions. Travelers come for Li River cruises, reed-flute caves, and nearby Yangshuo’s biking and rafting landscapes.
hls: Li River boat journeys to Yangshuo
Two Rivers and Four Lakes night views

todo.name: Li River cruise to Yangshuo
todo.desc: Take the classic route past limestone pinnacles, fishing villages, and postcard river bends.

todo.name: Reed Flute Cave visit
todo.desc: Walk illuminated chambers filled with stalactites and dramatic rock formations.

todo.name: Elephant Trunk Hill viewpoint
todo.desc: See Guilin’s best-known natural symbol where rock arches resemble an elephant drinking from the river.

todo.name: Cycle Yangshuo countryside
todo.desc: Rent bikes for village roads between karst towers, bamboo groves, and river crossings.

todo.name: Longji Rice Terraces day trip
todo.desc: Head into the mountains for layered paddies and minority villages, especially striking in planting and harvest seasons.

todo.name: Evening Two Rivers and Four Lakes cruise
todo.desc: Enjoy lit bridges and waterfront landmarks after dark in central Guilin.

wifi.note: Hotel Wi-Fi is common, but some global apps require roaming solutions or approved VPN access.

prac.fltNote: From Gatwick, routes usually connect via major hubs (e.g., Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, or Doha) before onward domestic service to Guilin.
prac.visa: China entry rules vary by nationality and transit policy; apply for the correct visa or confirm current visa-free transit eligibility before travel.
alerts: National holiday weeks (especially early October) bring heavy domestic travel demand.

sweet: October to December is generally the most comfortable; spring is green but wetter, and summer is hot and humid.
